vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-1164: Push diff summary updates over /ws/rpc instead of 5s polling

plan.title: Push diff summary updates over /ws/rpc instead of 5s polling
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-7

narratives.Problem: ConversationPanel polls `/api/conversations/:name/diffs` every 5s via `refetchInterval: 5000`. After Claude completes a turn, the new file edit can take 5-6s end-to-end to appear in the 'Changed files' header — users who open the diff panel during that gap see a stale or empty list. The polling also runs unconditionally for every open panel, regardless of whether anything changed.
narratives.Proposal: Push diff-summary updates as in-memory domain events over the existing `/ws/rpc` Effect RPC stream. Server side: when `getCachedMessages` finishes parsing JSONL on a messages poll, diff the new `fileEditsByAssistantId` against the cached snapshot, compute a per-turn `TurnDiffSummary` for any new/changed assistant turn (via a helper extracted from the existing diffs route), and fire-and-forget `getEventStore().emitOnly({ type: 'conversation.diff_summary_updated', ... })` with the full summary payload. Frontend side: add a reducer that maintains `conversationDiffSummariesByName: Record<string, TurnDiffSummary[]>` in the dashboard store; ConversationPanel reads from the store, keeps the on-mount HTTP fetch (seeded into React Query) for initial state, and drops `refetchInterval: 5000` entirely. End-to-end propagation drops from ~5s to ~2s (bounded by the existing messages poll, ~1s average), with no extra polling traffic.

items.title: Extract per-turn diff computation into a shared helper

narrative.Action: Extract the per-assistant-turn diff-computation block currently inlined in `getConversationDiffsRoute` (src/dashboard/server/routes/conversations.ts lines ~2585-2683) into a reusable helper, e.g. `computeConversationTurnDiffSummary(opts: { assistantId, edits, assistantMessage, conv, repoRootCache, baseCommitCache }): Promise<TurnDiffSummaryWithFiles | null>` in a new file `src/dashboard/server/services/conversation-diff-summary.ts` (or alongside `conversation-service.ts`). The helper takes one assistant message's edits, returns a single summary (or null if no diff). The HTTP route loops over `fileEditsByAssistantId` and calls the helper for each entry — its public response is byte-identical to today. The emit hook (next bead) will reuse the same helper for the new turn(s) only.

subItems.title: Helper function is exported from a new (or existing) services module and accepts `repoRootCache` + `baseCommitCache` as inputs so the caller controls cache lifetime
`getConversationDiffsRoute` is refactored to call the helper inside its loop; the HTTP response shape, ordering, and contents are unchanged (snapshot test or manual byte-compare)

Unit test in `src/dashboard/server/__tests__/conversation-diff-summary.test.ts` covers (a) successful per-file numstat parsing, (b) empty-edit input returns null, (c) repo-resolution failure path returns null
All git/exec calls are async (`promisify(exec)`); no `execSync` or other blocking calls introduced (per CLAUDE.md `no-execsync-server` rule)
`npm run typecheck`, `npm run lint`, and `npm test` all pass

items.title: Add `conversation.diff_summary_updated` event to contracts and reducer

narrative.Action: Wire the new domain event end-to-end in `packages/contracts/`. (1) In `events.ts`, define `ConversationDiffSummaryUpdatedEvent` next to the other `ConversationXxx` events with payload `{ conversationName, assistantMessageId, completedAt, files: TurnDiffFileChange[] }`. Mark in the JSDoc as in-memory-only (mirrors `ConversationCompactingChangedEvent`). Add it to the `DomainEvent` schema union. (2) In `event-reducers.ts`, extend `ReadModelState` with `conversationDiffSummariesByName: Record<string, TurnDiffSummary[]>` (default `{}`), update `initialReadModelState`, and add a reducer case that builds a `TurnDiffSummary` (using `turnId = \`conv-turn-${assistantMessageId}\`` to match the HTTP route's id scheme), deduplicates by `assistantMessageId` (replace-if-exists, append-otherwise), and trims via the existing `trimTurnDiffSummaries`. (3) Rebuild contracts: `cd packages/contracts && npm run build`. (4) Add reducer test mirroring the `agent.turn_diff_completed` test.

subItems.title: `ConversationDiffSummaryUpdatedEvent` is defined in `events.ts` with `type: 'conversation.diff_summary_updated'`, payload schema as specified, and exported type
The event is added to the `DomainEvent` union and the contracts package builds (`cd packages/contracts && npm run build`)

`ReadModelState.conversationDiffSummariesByName: Record<string, TurnDiffSummary[]>` is added, defaulted in `initialReadModelState`, and the reducer dedupes by `assistantMessageId` (replace-if-exists, append-otherwise)
Reducer test asserts: (a) first event appends a new summary; (b) second event for the same `assistantMessageId` replaces in-place; (c) events for different `conversationName`s do not cross-pollute
`getSnapshot` returns `conversationDiffSummariesByName` so reconnects preserve the state (verify in `DashboardSnapshot` schema or snapshot construction code)
`npm run typecheck`, `npm run lint`, and `npm test` all pass

narrative.Action: Hook the emit into `getCachedMessages` (src/dashboard/server/routes/conversations.ts). After the parse result is computed (incremental or full), compare its `fileEditsByAssistantId` against the previous cache snapshot's edits to find new or grown entries. For each new assistantId, look up the merged assistant message (skip if not present — guards against orphan UUIDs per H2), then fire-and-forget call the helper from bead `extract-diff-helper` and emit `getEventStore().emitOnly({ type: 'conversation.diff_summary_updated', ... })` with the resulting `TurnDiffSummary` fields. Pass `conversationName` into `getCachedMessages` (currently it only takes `sessionFile` + `isSpecialist`) — derive it from the route caller. Skip emit when `isSpecialist === true` (per H4) or when the conversation lookup fails. Maintain a per-`sessionFile` Map<assistantId, fileCount> in module scope to dedup across concurrent pollers (per H1) and clear it on cache eviction.

subItems.title: `getCachedMessages` accepts an optional `conversationName?: string` argument; when present and `isSpecialist === false`, the function performs the diff-detect-and-emit step after computing `result`
The `/api/conversations/:name/messages` route passes the conversation name into `getCachedMessages`; the `/api/conversations/:name/diffs` route and any other callers continue to work (compile + run)

Detect-and-emit logic is fire-and-forget (`void (async () => {...})()`) so the HTTP response is not delayed; the inner work uses `await` for git/exec calls and handles errors via try/catch + console.error (no unhandled rejections)

Per-`sessionFile` last-emit tracking dedupes events: the same assistantId with the same file count emits exactly once across multiple concurrent `/messages` polls (verified by an integration test that simulates two parallel calls)

Orphan tool_use UUIDs (those not present as keys in `result.messages.find(m => m.role === 'assistant')`) do NOT trigger an emit; once re-keying merges them into the real assistant message UUID, that real UUID DOES emit
Specialist sessions (`isSpecialist=true`) and sessions for which `getConversationByName` returns null do not emit (silent skip, no error)

Integration test in `src/dashboard/server/__tests__/`: append a tool_use entry for `Edit` to a fixture JSONL between two `getCachedMessages` calls; assert exactly one `conversation.diff_summary_updated` event is captured from `getEventStore()` with the correct `conversationName`, `assistantMessageId`, and `files[]`
`npm run typecheck`, `npm run lint`, `npm test`, and `npm run build` all pass

items.title: Consume store summaries in ConversationPanel and drop refetchInterval

narrative.Action: In `src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/chat/ConversationPanel.tsx`: (1) remove `refetchInterval: 5000` from the `conversation-diffs` useQuery (keep the on-mount fetch so older turns hydrate React Query). (2) Read `conversationDiffSummariesByName[conversation.name]` from the dashboard store via `useDashboardStore`. (3) Replace the current `turnDiffSummaryByAssistantMessageId` derivation so it merges store summaries (live deltas) over the React Query summaries (initial seed), then maps by `assistantMessageId` using the same fallback timestamp-matching logic. (4) Optional polish: after the initial HTTP fetch resolves, write its summaries into the store too so the merge is naturally deduped — or leave them separate and merge at read time. Pick the simpler one. Verify the diff click-through (`handleOpenTurnDiff`) is unchanged.

subItems.title: `refetchInterval: 5000` is removed from the `['conversation-diffs', conversation.name]` useQuery in `ConversationPanel.tsx`; the on-mount fetch still runs once
ConversationPanel subscribes to `conversationDiffSummariesByName[conversation.name]` from the dashboard store; new summaries appear in the 'Changed files' header without any additional HTTP fetch

Existing click-through behavior — opening a per-file turn diff via `handleOpenTurnDiff` and the `diffUrlPrefix` — is unchanged; the `TurnDiffPanel` still loads from `/api/conversations/:name/diffs/:turnId`

Component unit test (Vitest + RTL) renders ConversationPanel with mocked store + React Query, dispatches a `conversation.diff_summary_updated` event into the store, asserts the new file appears in the rendered DOM without invalidating the React Query cache
`npm run typecheck`, `npm run lint`, and `npm test` (root + frontend) all pass

items.title: Playwright UAT — live edit propagates within ~1s, no /diffs polling

narrative.Action: Add a Playwright UAT in `src/dashboard/frontend/tests/` that exercises the acceptance criteria from the issue end-to-end using an isolated browser instance (no shared profile/session per the Panopticon isolation rule). Test flow: (a) spin up dashboard + create or attach to a test conversation with a known JSONL fixture path; (b) open the ConversationPanel and wait for initial load; (c) record network requests via `page.on('request')`; (d) append a synthetic `Edit` tool_use entry to the JSONL file from the test harness; (e) assert the new file appears in the 'Changed files' header within 2.5s (covers the 2s messages poll + event roundtrip + render); (f) over the following 6s, assert there is exactly ONE `/api/conversations/:name/diffs` GET request — the on-mount fetch — and zero periodic refetches. The test uses its own browser context (`browser.newContext()`) and does not depend on any other agent's Playwright session.

subItems.title: Playwright test file lives under `src/dashboard/frontend/tests/conversation-diff-push.spec.ts` and runs as part of the dashboard frontend test command
Test uses an isolated `browser.newContext()` — does not rely on any other agent's Playwright session, profile, or login state
Appending a tool_use Edit entry to the test JSONL causes the new file path to render in the 'Changed files' header within 2.5s of the JSONL write
Over a 6-second observation window after the panel opens, exactly one HTTP GET to `/api/conversations/:name/diffs` is observed (the on-mount fetch) — no periodic polling
Clicking a file in the 'Changed files' header still opens the per-turn unified diff (existing behavior unchanged)
Test passes locally and in the project's standard test command (`npm test` or whichever entry runs Playwright frontend tests)
